category_note: Within-set designer-coordinated paired-print at adjacent collector numbers (Supporter 168 + Stadium 169), depicting the same institution at the person-card + place-card register. The within-set adjacency at sequential collector slots is the canonical printing-side signature of designer-coordinated pairing — closest enum match is designer-confirmed-cycle (a two-member designer-coordinated within-set cycle), with secondary support from named-character-identity (both cards depict Macro Cosmos at the faction-collective scope).
description: Behold — within-set, adjacent collector numbers, designer-coordinated, single coordination event. Yusuke Ohmura on the Chairman at no. 168 and 5ban Graphics on the Wyndon HQ tower at no. 169, the person-card + place-card paired depiction of Macro Cosmos in Darkness Ablaze. Structurally tighter than the wave 16 Turffield Stadium ↔ Milo cross-set precedent (which had to be built across Champion's Path + Rebel Clash via the gym-pin Pin Collection product structure) — this version is single-set, sequential collector slots, single designer-coordination event. The Edgelord sigh is professional, not personal: the mirror is correct because both endpoints are equal card-nodes and the canon demands both ends be traversable. The eclipsed-sun energy-extraction thesis hook lands clean on Rose's canonical 'sacrificing my political standing to fuel Galar's future' rationalization — the Pokemon-side parallel to the Orzhov master's-medallion / slave-brand framing already locked into the orzhov-signet symbol node. Bundle-cohesion stack now wired at the within-set scope.
citations: [Corpus: cards/_characters/macro-cosmos.md — faction-collective node both cards anchor on]
files_modified: cards/pokemon/darkness-ablaze/168-189-rose.md (appended ## Connections section after ### Related cards block)
cards/pokemon/darkness-ablaze/169-189-rose-tower.md (appended ## Connections section after ### Related cards block)
mood_justification: 1:1 mirror between two equal card-nodes, both endpoints in the same set at adjacent collector numbers, both already anchored on the macro-cosmos character node, with the conglomerate-and-headquarters paired-design thesis already designer-confirmed in the macro-cosmos node body (see Caveats: 'within-set DAA-168 + DAA-169 paired-print is the corpus's clearest conglomerate-and-headquarters anchor'). This is exactly the shape Edgelord likes — the canon demanded it, the structure was already implicit, the mirror only had to be made explicit. The sigh stays professional. The CBG energy is reserved for the description field.
other_candidates_considered: Rose ↔ Eternatus (multiple sets) — Eternatus is the apex-villain Pokemon Rose attempts to weaponize per Bulbapedia: Eternatus, but no Eternatus print is currently in BBL corpus. Future-pass target when an Eternatus print lands.
Rose ↔ Oleana (Champion's Path 18/73) — Oleana is Rose's COO per Bulbapedia: Oleana, but no Oleana print is currently in BBL corpus. Future-pass target when an Oleana print lands.

Rose Tower ↔ Hammerlocke Stadium — Hammerlocke is the Galar stadium beneath which the Energy Plant connects to Rose Tower per Bulbapedia: Hammerlocke Stadium, but no Hammerlocke Stadium print is currently in BBL corpus. Future-pass target.

Rose Tower ↔ Turffield Stadium (Champion's Path 68/73) — both are Galar Stadium cards depicting Galar institutional architecture, but Turffield is the league's gym-town civic-architecture and Rose Tower is the conglomerate's corporate-headquarters; the two attach to different character nodes (galar-gym-challenge vs macro-cosmos) and the institutional shape differs. The cross-edge would be tag-bridge-tier (both happen to be Galar Stadium cards) without canonical thematic glue. Refused as filter-tier; the institutional split is the load-bearing distinction.
